channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on comms box on The Avenue, near Apex Close, 5th June
item.description:
The comms box between 44 The Avenue and Apex Close on the south side of The Avenue has some graffiti on it. Please can it be removed - thanks!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bike dumped, 5th June
item.description:
Dumped Forest bike on the footway. I do not believe that Forest bikes operate in the Greenwich borough

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Sherard Road, Well Hall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian footpaths/ pavements, 5th June
item.description:
Cars constantly parked on pedestrian footpaths, pavements blocking access for wheelchair users, those with pushchairs or those carrying loads.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Watling Gardens, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In the very middle of Belsize Terrace, centre of the road outside Aperivino bar or Mimi restaurant, 5th June
item.description:
There is water seeping through cracks in the tarmac, likely from a leaking pipe underneath. The tarmac is always wet, even in the dry weather.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10A, Belsize Terrace, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous stump, 5th June
item.description:
I am assuming this is going to be removed soon? Blocking the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shoreham Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Lime Bikes, 5th June
item.description:
These have fallen over in the wind A resident has already complained on 3rd June They are on footpath Point Hill/Maidenstone Hill but as of today they’ve been there a week! One bike plate is PPJEBS. This should help identify location. The resident was assured you would dispatch someone to collect? Unacceptable this has not happened

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maidenstone Hill, East Greenwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bed and rubbish, 5th June
item.description:
Bed and rubbish dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Windus Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 Canisters found in the parking area, 5th June
item.description:
2 canisters were found left on the ground in this parking area. We have moved them to the trash area of the apartments so they are not in the way of the cars, as this is seriously dangerous. We understand that this is not a way to dispose of it, but we don't know where these have come from so we don't feel comfortable taking it to the waste disposal area ourselves hence we want to let you know

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenhaven Drive, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shelf cupboard and supermarket trolley, 5th June
item.description:
Shelf cupboard and supermarket trolley were dumped here a few weeks ago.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bewick Mews, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Excessive litter and dirt in the alleyway, 5th June
item.description:
There’s been an ongoing littering issue in the alleyway, with nearby restaurants and clubs dumping all kinds of rubbish. It’s been like this for weeks and it’s very unhygienic. Please investigate who is responsible, the situation is completely out of order

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shacklewell Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Metal sticking up, 5th June
item.description:
Dangerous metal sticking up from ground by bus stop D

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plumstead Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illuminated bollard - damaged - squashed, 5th June
item.description:
B1614
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 63, Hayes Lane, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree with damaged branch about to fall on pavement, 5th June
item.description:
Broken branch needs cutting and taking away
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elm Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 5th June
item.description:
Dead fox in park, poor little thing is on his back - hopefully not a sign of foul play as he doesn’t seem to have collapsed on his front

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 23, Leesons Way, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped car, 5th June
item.description:
This is the third time I have reported this vehicle it's been 12 weeks , it is untaxed and no MOT which makes it illegal to be on the road so why is it still here? its a white Vauxhall Astra WF10LBA . so much for Bromley Council removing dumped cars.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Balfour Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard lying loose on its side in Crook Log, DA6 8EG, 5th June
item.description:
One of the bollards on the crossing island lying loose on its side for a while now, in Crook Log, DA6 8EG.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crook Log, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Table is broken, 5th June
item.description:
Park's table is unstable, out of place. Legs need to be welded back into place

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-7, Rotherhithe Street, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - shelter unstable or loose in the ground

item.title: Builders waste, 5th June
item.description:
Bag of builders waste abandoned on the pavement by the lamp post.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lytchet Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Left equipment, 5th June
item.description:
JB riney have left equipment on the callers road blocking drives and traffice, caller advises they have been left in a very dangerous postion and are impeading traffic outside 12 Cockmannings Rd, Orpington
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cockmannings Road, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
